items.title: Anwar's Russia, Turkmenistan visits helped lower diesel prices, says Fahmi
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The reduction in subsidised diesel prices announced last week is a direct dividend of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's working visits to Russia and Turkmenistan, said unity government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. Fahmi said the Kazan leg of the trip secured a 20-year energy security agreement covering crude oil, gas and diesel, while the Ashgabat visit saw Petronas awarded two gas wells to manage and develop, cementing its place among the world's largest gas operators. He said these energy security gains were closely tied to Anwar's June 21 announcement of a cut in the price of subsidised diesel.

items.title: Hong Leong issues RM640mil Tier 2 subordinated notes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hong Leong Bank Bhd (HLB) has issued subordinated notes worth RM640 million as part of its multi-currency Tier 2 subordinated notes programme. HLB said Tranche 7 Series 1 has a 10-year tenure with a five-year non-call period, while Tranche 7 Series has a 12-year tenure with a seven-year non-call period. HLB said the Tranche 7 Series 1 call date is June 26, 2031, and Tranche 7 Series 2 is June 27, 2033, with every coupon payment date thereafter.

items.title: Fahmi: Opposition support needed for legal reform bill
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government is counting on opposition support to pass a landmark bill separating the roles of attorney-general and public prosecutor, said government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. 2) Bill 2026, which Fahmi described as a major reform the government is committed to delivering. "At the federal level, we are aware the Unity Government remains intact, and support from component parties continues to allow the Madani government to function effectively," he said.

items.title: UN says Iran nuclear pledge needs 'very strong' verification
items.summary: TEHERAN: The UN's nuclear chief said Friday that Iran's pledge not to build a nuclear weapon would need "very strong" verification, as the United States and the Islamic republic negotiate a permanent settlement to end their war. Iran's nuclear programme is a key sticking point in talks to end the Middle East war, which began in late February with massive US-Israeli strikes. Grossi said the UN's nuclear watchdog had so far "barely initiated" talks with Iran following the agreement with the United States.

items.title: Mercedes tightens cost-cutting measures, postpones special payment
items.summary: STUTTGART: German car manufacturer Mercedes-Benz is tightening its cost-cutting measures, with the DAX-listed group postponing a special payment under the collective agreement, originally scheduled for July, until next year, German Press Agency (dpa) reported. The company stated that structural costs in Germany – particularly labour costs – are not competitive by international standards. Under the collective agreement, employees currently work 35 hours a week.

items.title: Civil service to adopt hybrid work from Aug 1
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Cabinet has agreed to implement the Hybrid Working Day (HBH) initiative as a new norm across the civil service, effective Aug 1. According to a Public Services Department (PSD) circular, the initiative introduces a more flexible working arrangement for civil servants without reducing their total working hours. Under the policy, eligible civil servants may work from home or other approved locations for two days a week, while spending the remaining three days in the office.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 26, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed slightly higher on Friday after a late rebound in banking stocks pushed the benchmark index into positive territory. Telecommunications stocks in the FBM KLCI continued to gain support, with steady buying interest throughout the session. Although banking stocks were mostly lower during the day, a strong late recovery helped lift the overall market at the close.

items.title: Labuan Airport shuts runway after bombs found near tarmac, flights cancelled and diverted until clearance
items.summary: LABUAN, June 26 — Flight operations at Labuan Airport have been disrupted following the temporary closure of its runway after the discovery of bombs 150-metre from the runway. A Notice to Airmen (NOTAM), as posted at the Malaysia Airport Facebook, has been issued by the Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) at 5pm today for the temporary closure of the runway until further notice. Bernama’s checks at the Labuan Airport found that flights from 4.05pm, 5.15pm, 6.05pm, 6.15pm and 7.25pm destined to Kuala Lumpur, Kota Kinabalu and Miri have been affected.

items.title: Econpile wins RM48.8mil Pavilion serviced apartment job
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Econpile Holdings Bhd has secured a RM48.8 million contract to undertake specialist bored pile and foundation works for a 74-storey serviced apartment development along Jalan Pavilion here. The contract was awarded by Vestland Bhd's subsidiary Vestland Infra Sdn Bhd, to Econpile's unit Econpile (M) Sdn Bhd. Econpile executive director and group chief executive officer Raymond Pang said surpassing RM400 million in new contract wins in FY26 reflects the industry's confidence in the group's specialised geotechnical expertise.

items.title: Helicopter services specialist GTA gets Bursa nod for listing
items.summary: According to its exposure prospectus, the proposed initial public offering (IPO) will involve 329 million shares, comprising 205 million new shares and 124 million existing shares offered for sale. Of the new shares, 64.57 million will be made available to the public, 12.91 million allocated to eligible directors and employees, while 127.52 million shares will be placed out to institutional and selected investors. Revenue contributions came from proactive maintenance service packages at RM91.79 million, corrective maintenance works at RM50.19 million and aviation equipment sales amounting to RM94.67 million.

items.title: Thai police foil bid to smuggle RM1.2mil drugs through Malaysia
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Thai authorities have arrested 233 suspected members of drug trafficking syndicates, some believed to have links with Malaysian traffickers, during a month-long anti-drug crackdown in Narathiwat province. Dubbed "Narathiwat Fights Drugs", the operation targeted 92 locations across the southern Thai province, including areas bordering Malaysia. The operation also targeted drug abuse, with action taken against 1,913 drug users.

items.title: Customs bust RM52.9m AI server syndicate at KLIA Free Trade Zone, probe under Strategic Trade Act
items.summary: SEPANG, June 26 — The Customs Department (Customs) has seized 72 servers containing advanced artificial intelligence (AI) chips, valued at an estimated RM52.9 million, at the Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Free Trade Zone on suspicion of violating the Strategic Trade Act 2010. KLIA Customs director Zulkifli Muhammad said the seizure was made during an inspection by a team from the KLIA Customs Department’s Enforcement Division at a warehouse in the KLIA Free Trade Zone at about 4.30pm on June 5. Zulkifli said all 72 servers had been seized for further investigation as they were suspected of being subject to transactions involving unlisted items and restricted activities under the Strategic Trade Act 2010.

items.title: Anwar: Civil service pension payments set to hit RM46bil by 2030
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is fine-tuning a new retirement scheme for civil servants, as pension payments are expected to reach RM46.36 billion by 2030. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the proposed new mechanism for the civil service retirement scheme, known as the "contributory permanent appointment" scheme, is still being studied. Hishammuddin had asked the government to state the fiscal implications of the new civil service remuneration scheme and whether the restructuring of the pension scheme for new appointments would affect the civil service's ability to attract top talent.

items.title: Ringgit rises further versus US dollar, major and regional currencies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit has continued to strengthen against the US dollar, other major currencies and ASEAN peers on Friday, shrugging off the impact of stronger-than-expected United States (US) economic data. SPI Asset Management managing partner Stephen Innes said the latest US economic data revealed a more uneven picture beneath the headline figures, with growth driven primarily by capital expenditure and government spending, while consumer spending was sharply lower. "This has encouraged investors to trim aggressive US Federal Reserve (Fed) interest rate hike expectations, pulling US yields lower and taking pressure off Asian currencies, including the ringgit," he told Bernama.

items.title: Tengku Putri Arafiah, sister of Sultan Sharafuddin, dies at 72, laid to rest at Shah Alam mausoleum
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 26 — Tengku Putri Arafiah Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah, the younger sister of the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, who died at her residence this morning, was laid to rest at the Shah Alam Royal Mausoleum here at about 3.30pm today. Sultan Sharafuddin and the Raja Muda of Selangor, Tengku Amir Shah, attended the funeral prayers before witnessing the burial. The Selangor ruler was accompanied by the Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor, Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin, at the burial.

items.title: Campaign with respect, don't be arrogant, Anwar tells parties
items.summary: DENGKIL: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reminded all parties contesting the Johor election to campaign respectfully, uphold good values and not act arrogantly. Anwar said three-cornered or multi-cornered contests are a normal feature of a democratic system, but stressed that all parties must ensure their campaigns are carried out respectfully and with regard for the people. Anwar said all parties should adopt a moderate approach throughout the campaign period, prioritising cooperation and efforts to engage with the public.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia ends slightly higher as late rebound in banking stocks lifts market
items.summary: Among heavyweights, Maybank rose 8.0 sen to RM10.80, Public Bank and CIMB Group added 2.0 sen each to RM4.83 and RM7.41 respectively, Tenaga Nasional perked up 10 sen to RM14.34, and IHH Healthcare was 3.0 sen easier at RM8.54. As for the active counters, Zetrix AI was flat at 77 sen, Widad shed half a sen to 1.0 sen, AirAsia X slipped 5.0 sen to RM1.18, Dagang NeXchange lost 2.5 sen to 42 sen, and Tanco was flat to 13.5 sen. On the gainers' list, United Plantations ticked up 30 sen to RM32.60, Nestle rose 22 sen to RM95.84, Hong Leong Financial Group and Petronas Dagangan climbed 20 sen each to RM18.50 and RM18.60 respectively, and Solarvest jumped 14 sen to RM2.99. For the top losers, MPI fell RM2.00 to RM47.00, UMS Integration dipped 70 sen to RM7.80, Vitrox slipped 59 sen to RM7.26, F&N slid 48 sen to RM27.70, and UWC shed 30 sen to RM6.30.

items.title: Healthcare committee to review PAC findings on rising costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Joint Ministerial Committee on Private Healthcare Costs said it welcomes a parliamentary report on rising insurance premiums and private hospital charges and will give it "careful consideration" as reforms continue. The committee, co-chaired by Finance Minister II Senator Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan and Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad, said in a statement that the findings underline the urgency of ongoing healthcare reforms. The committee said it will give them "careful consideration as it continues to expedite reforms already underway."

items.title: Vestland unit bags RM135mil 'superstructure' contract in Klang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Vestland Bhd's subsidiary Vestland Resources Sdn Bhd has clinched a RM135 million construction contract from Golden Armani Sdn Bhd. The contract covers the design, approval, construction, completion, testing and commissioning of the superstructure works for the industrial development project. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Malaysians in Tokyo advised to remain vigilant following earthquake in chiba prefecture
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Embassy of Malaysia in Tokyo has advised Malaysians in Japan to remain vigilant and closely monitor local conditions following the magnitude 5.8 earthquake that struck off the northeastern coast of Chiba Prefecture this afternoon. The embassy, in an earthquake advisory posted on its Facebook page, also advised Malaysians in Japan to refer to the website www.jma.go.jp/jma/indexe.html for the latest updates, warnings and advisories issued by the Japan Meteorological Agency (JMA). "In the event of an emergency, please contact the Embassy of Malaysia in Tokyo office at +81-3-3476-3840 (office hours), the emergency hotline at +81-80-4322-3366 (after office hours), or email consular.tyo@kln.gov.my or mwtokyo@kln.gov.my," the advisory read.

items.title: RM135.63mil in drugs seized, bid to flood local market foiled
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police seized drugs worth an estimated RM135.63 million following three raids and the arrest of seven suspects in Cheras on June 20. The operation, codenamed Op Horitus, crippled a syndicate believed to have been processing and distributing narcotics to the local market since late last year. He said police also confiscated assets worth RM644,000, including six vehicles believed to be linked to the syndicate.

items.title: Petronas manager’s alleged leak of confidential report to Petros could jeopardise talks, court told
items.summary: Petronas General Manager Syakirah Mustapa, 51, said that if reports regarding Petronas’s operational and financial performance were leaked to a competitor, they would be able to know the current operational and financial performance of Petronas Upstream for the first quarter, as well as the overall estimates for the year 2024. “The leak of this information could jeopardize the negotiation process between Petronas and Petros, potentially leading to negotiations that are disadvantageous to Petronas,” she said when reading her witness statement during the trial of Mohd Khairul Akmal Mohd Jasni, who is charged with attempting to disclose the report to Petros. I am not sure if Mohd Khairul Akmal knows both individuals,” Syakirah said during the examination-in-chief by Deputy Public Prosecutor Noor Syafina Mohd Radzuan.

items.title: [UPDATED] Johor BN unveils manifesto with 63 pledges, 200,000 jobs target
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional (BN) today unveiled its manifesto for the Johor election, outlining 63 pledges anchored on six pillars aimed at preserving stability and sustaining the state's development momentum. The manifesto, themed "Maju Johor, Kestabilan Dikekalkan, Kemajuan Diteruskan" (Advancing Johor, Stability Preserved, Progress Continued), was formulated based on the coalition's experience in administering Johor over the past four years. Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the coalition's track record had laid the foundation for the manifesto.

items.title: Resilient dollar takes a step back, markets still wary of yen intervention risk
items.summary: LONDON: The dollar was a touch softer against other major currencies on Friday as fresh economic data and Federal Reserve comments led markets to pare rate-hike bets, allowing the yen trading in an intervention danger zone to find firmer ground. Thursday's data showing a key measure of US inflation met economists' expectations which tempered rate-hike bets. The euro was 0.15 per cent higher at US$1.1385, while the British pound was up 0.1 per cent at US$1.3201.

items.title: Johor BN promises 200,000 quality jobs, RM100m housing and education funds in state election manifesto
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 26 — Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) today pledged to create 200,000 quality jobs and channel RM100 million each towards affordable housing and youth education as it unveiled its manifesto for the 16th Johor state election. The manifesto, themed “ Maju Johor , Stability Maintained, Progress Continued”, contains 63 pledges built around six Maju Johor pillars, which Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said were based on the coalition’s four years in government and the needs of the people. “Come, Bangsa Johor !

items.title: 'Datuk' gang leader among 291 arrested in RM6.26mil drug bust [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A man with the title "Datuk", believed to be the mastermind of a drug trafficking syndicate, was arrested with 290 others in a major operation across Perak, Johor and Kuala Lumpur. Bukit Aman Narcotics Crime Investigation Department (NCID) Director, Datuk Hussein Omar Khan, said the operation initially focused on six districts in Perak, which are Taiping, Ipoh, Batu Gajah, Tapah, Manjung, and Hilir Perak, before hitting Kuala Lumpur and Johor. Hussein said 291 syndicate members were detained, including 21 people who are suspected of involvement in secret society activities.

items.title: Govt aims to table AG-Public Prosecutor separation Bill this parliamentary sitting, seeks bipartisan backing, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — The government is targeting the current parliamentary sitting to table a Bill for debate and approval on the proposed separation of the roles of the Attorney General and the Public Prosecutor, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said today. He said Putrajaya remains firmly committed to the proposed reform, which is seen as a significant step towards strengthening institutional independence and governance. He said lawmakers must prioritise the country’s democratic institutions over political considerations, emphasising that the reform would ensure the Public Prosecutor is free from executive influence.

items.title: Family mourns boy, 5, who fell from Penang condominium
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A family is mourning its 5-year-old son, who allegedly fell from a condominium in Teluk Kumbar, after a final light-hearted moment together. The family of the boy, believed to have fallen from the 24th floor of a condominium on Thursday night, described him as cheerful, affectionate and full of life. His father, Efwan Reza Rusdizal, 40, said Emyr Nader Redza, the youngest of three siblings, was a dominant yet sensitive child who often brightened the family's days with his playful personality.

items.title: Anwar orders local authority approval processes streamlined to boost Malaysia’s competitiveness
items.summary: DENGKIL, June 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim wants all approval processes and bureaucratic procedures at the local authority (PBT) level to be expedited and streamlined to enhance Malaysia’s global competitiveness. According to Anwar, differences in procedures between municipal councils and city councils have resulted in lengthy approval processes for projects such as housing and factory construction, driving up costs for applicants. He said several new measures would be introduced to speed up approval processes and administrative procedures at the PBT level, in line with the federal government’s objectives.

items.title: Tax exemption on services charges removes major cost concern: Industry body
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The service tax exemption on service charges and sinking fund contributions for non-residential buildings removes a major cost concern for industry stakeholders, said an industry body. The Malaysian Institute of Property and Facility Managers (MIPFM) said the exemption reduces operating costs for property owners, joint management bodies and building tenants, while providing greater certainty in planning operational and maintenance expenditure. If a building collects RM1 million annually in maintenance charges and sinking fund contributions, property owners would save RM80,000 under the service tax exemption.

items.title: Fahmi: PM Anwar directs Home Ministry to review Rohingya management findings after inter-agency meetings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has instructed the Home Ministry (KDN) to review several findings arising from a series of inter-agency coordination meetings on the management of the Rohingya community in Malaysia. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the directive was issued during today’s Cabinet meeting following discussions involving ministries and enforcement agencies. “The prime minister instructed KDN to examine several of the findings and to coordinate them with the relevant ministries, including the Foreign Ministry, as well as enforcement agencies.

items.title: Labuan fire and rescue operations save RM528.59m in property value so far this year
items.summary: LABUAN, June 26 — The Labuan Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) recorded an estimated RM528.59 million in property saved through fire and rescue operations between Jan 1 and June 22 this year, underscoring the need to enhance community fire safety and emergency preparedness. He said the opening of the Tanjung Aru PBS station was in line with JBPM’s aspiration to strengthen volunteer fire brigades nationwide, supporting its target of establishing 500 volunteer fire brigades by 2030. During the ceremony, JBPM also handed over fire prevention equipment to Community Fire units and firefighting equipment to the Tanjung Aru Volunteer Fire Brigade to further strengthen community capabilities in fire safety and emergency response.

items.title: Psychiatrist encounters increasing number of depressed and high risks children
items.summary: The 75th witness was replying to lawyer Shahlan Jufri's question on his additional training in Child and Adolescent Psychiatry. Shahlan is representing Zara Qairina's mother Noraidah Lamat. He said between 10 and 15 per cent involve those with autism; about 30 per cent are those with attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder; 30 to 40 per cent are those with depression and anxiety disorder; at least 30 per cent suffer from self-harm behaviours, including self-cutting; 15 to 20 per cent involve those with suicidal thoughts or behaviour; five to eight per cent are those with aggressive or violent behaviour; and more than 80 per cent involve those who refuse to attend school, have emotional difficulties or behavioural difficulties.

items.title: Federal Court rejects Isa Samad’s final bid to overturn RM3m graft conviction
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Former Felda chairman Datuk Seri Mohd Isa Abdul Samad has exhausted his legal avenues to overturn his conviction over RM3 million in kickbacks linked to the purchase of a hotel in Kuching, after the Federal Court today dismissed his bid to review an earlier ruling that upheld his conviction. A three-member Federal Court bench chaired by Chief Judge of Sabah and Sarawak Datuk Azizah Nawawi dismissed Isa’s application to review the court’s previous decision and to order a rehearing of his appeal. Azizah ruled that there had been no miscarriage of justice warranting the court’s intervention under Rule 137 of the Federal Court Rules 1995.

items.title: Babysitter remanded three days over death of 11-month-old girl in Bedong [WATCH]
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: A babysitter has been remanded for three days to assist investigations into the death of an 11-month-old girl. Media reports yesterday said the babysitter was detained by police after being called in to assist investigations into the death of the infant, who had been under her care at her home in Taman Permaipura, Bedong. The baby girl died at 10.42am yesterday while receiving treatment at Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital (HSB) in Alor Setar.

items.title: Fahmi: Modest but festive National Day celebration planned this year
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA:The government will stage a more modest National Day celebration this year without compromising the festive spirit, while unveiling 'Kesejahteraan Dinikmati Malaysia Madani' as the official theme for the 2026 celebrations. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the main event on Aug 31, 2026, will be held at Dataran Putrajaya as the government seeks to organise a celebration that remains festive while adopting a more modest approach. "I am pleased to announce that the 2026 National Day celebration will be held at Dataran Putrajaya on a more modest scale, but it will not be any less festive," he said.

items.title: Samenta calls for shift from welfare to innovation-led SME support
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia should shift its approach to supporting small and medium enterprises (SMEs), with greater emphasis on innovation, productivity and competitiveness, the Small and Medium Enterprises Association Malaysia (Samenta) said. Its national president, Datuk William Ng, said it was time to stop treating SMEs as welfare recipients and instead build an ecosystem that rewards value creation and long-term growth. "GDP measures output.

items.title: Six children among 10 hurt in three-vehicle crash near Bentong
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Six children were among 10 people injured in a three-vehicle crash along Jalan Mempaga–Raub near Kem Smart BKERP in Kampung Lebu, Bentong, today. Pahang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant director of operations Mohd Salahuddin Isa said two victims suffered relatively serious injuries to their waist and legs, while two other occupants of one of the vehicles escaped unhurt. Mohd Salahuddin said the victims comprised three men, three women and six children, including three girls.

items.title: Husband recounts missed goodbye before wife's fatal crash
items.summary: GERIK: "Every morning before leaving for work, we would shake hands and kiss each other goodbye. She died at the scene together with Inspector Melvindar Singh Gamum Singh, 41, of the Pengkalan Hulu district police headquarters. Five other teachers travelling with Zuraida were injured.

items.title: Anwar: Govt to build more housing for civil servants
items.summary: DENGKIL: The government will build more housing for civil servants on government-owned land at lower rental rates to help ease their cost of living burden, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said the move followed rising rental rates that have affected civil servants, particularly in major cities. "During my recent visits to Penang, Perak, Negri Sembilan, Johor and Kuala Lumpur, I found that civil servants are facing a serious problem — rising rental rates.

items.title: EC: 593 nomination forms sold ahead of Johor polls, but only 133 candidates pay deposits
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 26 — A total of 593 nomination forms have been sold for the Johor state election so far, but only 133 prospective candidates have formally confirmed their participation by paying the required election deposit, the Election Commission (EC) said today. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the number is expected to increase as prospective candidates still have until nomination proceedings begin tomorrow morning to complete the necessary formalities. “As of this morning, we have sold 593 nomination forms at Returning Officer (RO) offices throughout the state.

items.title: Anwar says no directive issued to stop Onn Hafiz entering Felda settlements
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today denied claims that anyone had been instructed to prevent Johor caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi from entering Felda settlements, saying the federal government only handles matters under its own jurisdiction. According to Berita Harian , Anwar said there had been no directive to bar Onn Hafiz from entering Felda areas after the Johor leader claimed he had been prevented from attending a land title presentation ceremony for settlers. Anwar was responding to Onn Hafiz’s allegation that he had been prevented from entering a Felda settlement to present land titles to settlers during the Johor State Felda Settlers’ Day 2026 celebration.

items.title: New low-rent housing projects planned for civil servants nationwide, says Anwar
items.summary: DENGKIL, June 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government will develop housing for civil servants with lower rental rates as an effort to look after the welfare of the group. “We will develop housing for civil servants with cheap rent. Anwar said that although the government had increased the salaries of civil servants, the issue of increasing rental rates is still a problem in several states.

items.title: Nine arrested as GOF uncovers RM3.75m illegal bauxite operation in Felda plantation
items.summary: Southeast Brigade GOF commander SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the 11.30am operation led to the arrest of four local men and five foreign men believed to be involved in illegal bauxite mining activities. “Preliminary investigations found that the five foreign nationals failed to produce valid work permits and identification documents. The operation involved the intelligence branch of the 7th Battalion GOF Kuantan, the Pahang State Enforcement Unit (UPNP), and the Criminal Investigation Division of the Kuantan District Police Headquarters.

items.title: Maserati, Alphard, Rolexes and RM6.3m in drugs seized in crackdown on alleged ‘Datuk’ kingpin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — A man holding the title ‘Datuk’, believed to be the mastermind of a drug syndicate, was among 291 individuals detained in Op Gaurus conducted by the Bukit Aman Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (JSJN) across Perak, the Klang Valley and Johor last week. Bukit Aman NCIID director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said the suspect, in his mid-40s, is believed to be the leader of a secret society active in the states. He said that in the operation carried out from June 17 to 21, police seized various types of drugs, including heroin, MDMA, ketamine, ecstasy pills, Erimin 5 pills and marijuana, with an estimated value of RM6.26 million, and arrested nine syndicate members.

items.title: Anwar says top approval rating is no excuse for complacency, vows to work harder
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said being named Malaysia’s most popular political leader should not make him complacent, insisting that public approval must instead spur him to improve his government’s performance. The poll placed returning Umno leader Khairy Jamaluddin second with a 50 per cent approval rating, followed by former prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin at 36 per cent. PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang and Umno president and Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi registered approval ratings of 25 per cent and 24 per cent respectively.

items.title: Four denied bail over alleged Jelutong temple attack sparked by false theft claim
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 26 — Four men pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to charges of being members of an unlawful assembly with the common intention of causing grievous hurt to a man last week. The accused, R. Nahveen Raaj, 34; S. Logakumaran, 31; M. Sanjit Kumar, 38; and K. Dineskumar, 30, were jointly charged with four other individuals who remain at large. They were accused of being members of an unlawful assembly in which one or more members allegedly caused grievous hurt to Mohamed Umar Sheriff Mohamed Ameen, 31.

items.title: MetMalaysia: Rain forecast across much of Johor on state election nomination day
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 26 — Rain is forecast to affect seven of the 10 districts in the state tomorrow morning, potentially posing a slight “challenge” for prospective candidates and supporters on nomination day for the Johor state election. Johor director of the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), Azlai Ta’at, said rain is expected in Johor Bahru, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kota Tinggi, Pontian, Tangkak and Kulai. Barisan Nasional is also contesting 56 seats, fielding 36 UMNO candidates, 16 MCA candidates and four MIC candidates.

items.title: Johor police announce road closures and diversions for state election nomination day
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 26 — Several major roads near nomination centres in the state will be closed or diverted in stages to facilitate nomination day for the 16th Johor state election tomorrow. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the closures and diversions are to ensure safety and smooth conduct of the nomination process. The roads include Jalan Utama Dato’ Onn and Jalan Ayer Molek in Johor Bahru, Jalan Persiaran Indahpura 6 in Kulai, Jalan Omar in Batu Pahat, Jalan Arab and Jalan Petri in Muar, and Jalan Dato’ Teoh Siew Khor and Jalan Sultan in Kluang.

items.title: Anwar urges parties to keep Johor campaign civil, cautions against arrogance ahead of polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today urged parties contesting the Johor state election to campaign with civility and humility, cautioning against arrogance even when promoting their achievements to voters. According to Utusan Malaysia , Anwar said fierce contests, including the prospect of three-cornered fights, were a normal part of democracy, but stressed that campaigning should remain respectful and guided by good values. The prime minister, who is also Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, said he had reminded the coalition’s election machinery not to become overconfident despite its track record.

items.title: Court forfeits Rolex watches, jewellery seized from ex-army chief’s sister-in-law to govt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — A total of 23 Rolex watches and 15 pieces of jewellery seized by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) from Nor Shahira Atirah Anuar @ Kamaruddin, the sister-in-law of former Army Chief Tan Sri Muhammad Hafizuddeain Jantan, were officially forfeited to the government today. Lawyer Muhammad Kausar Mohd Khairi, representing Nor Shahira Atirah, confirmed the outcome. In earlier proceedings, the MACC had applied for a court order to publish a gazette notice calling on any persons with an interest in the seized items to appear in court, pursuant to Section 41(2) of the MACC Act 2009.

items.title: Kuching court sentences Serian man who raped 15-year-old neighbour to 30 years’ jail, 18 lashes
items.summary: KUCHING, June 26 — The Sessions Court here has sentenced a 28-year-old man to a total of 30 years in prison and 18 strokes of the rotan for statutory rape. Suspecting that her daughter had been raped, the mother lodged a police report, prompting police action and a medical examination of the victim at hospital. Acting on information received, the police subsequently detained the accused on June 18.

items.title: Police seek saliva test kits for roadblocks to detect ‘Piu Piu’, ‘Magic Mushroom’ vape users
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Police are seeking to deploy saliva testing kits at roadblocks by the end of the year to detect motorists under the influence of synthetic drugs marketed as “Piu Piu” and “Magic Mushroom”, amid concerns the substances are contributing to road accidents. According to Berita Harian , Bukit Aman Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said the move comes as police have detected more cases involving motorists believed to have been impaired after inhaling synthetic drug liquids through electronic cigarettes or vape devices. Hussein explained that “Piu Piu” and “Magic Mushroom” are not the names of specific drugs but commercial terms used to market synthetic drug liquids mixed into vape devices.

items.title: Study finds Malaysian women often kept quiet about sexual violence over fears they would not be believed
items.summary: This means the study was on both Muslim and non-Muslim women, with the respondents also of various ethnicities: Malay (62 per cent); Chinese (21 per cent); other Bumiputera (10 per cent) and Indian (four per cent). Out of the 1,004 women aged 18 to 65 surveyed, 43 per cent said they had experienced sexual violence, with the most common types including groping, unwanted sexual comments and online sexual harassment. The most common places where these women experienced sexual violence were online (40 per cent), in public spaces (38 per cent), workplaces (21 per cent), homes (11 per cent), and educational institutions (7 per cent).

items.title: Selangor targets bigger share of Malaysia’s aerospace industry by 2030, says state exco
items.summary: SERDANG, June 26 — Selangor is aiming to further strengthen its position as Malaysia’s leading aerospace hub and a key regional player by expanding its maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) capabilities, developing skilled talent and enhancing its investment ecosystem. Ng said Selangor also aims to produce about 22,000 skilled aerospace workers by 2030 through investments in education and training to meet growing industry demand. The Selangor Aerospace Forum convenes today in conjunction with the MyAERO Summit 2026, Malaysia’s premier aerospace and future mobility event.

items.title: Psychiatrist tells inquest Zara Qairina endured mounting peer pressure before death
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 26 — The Coroner’s Court heard today that Zara Qairina Mahathir had endured repeated emotional pressure linked to peer relationships in the months leading up to her death. Dr Wong revealed that between March and May 2025, there were mounting signs that Zara Qairina was struggling with tension among peers, emotional insecurity, fear of confrontation, anxiety over senior students and reports of mocking or humiliating treatment. “By June and July 2025, the materials reflected progressive emotional distress, heightened rejection sensitivity, increased interpersonal vigilance and diminished perceived emotional safety,” he said, reading his witness statement during the inquest proceedings before Coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan.

items.title: Diesel-powered MPV owners should get extra 100 litres subsidy, says MAA
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — The Malaysian Automotive Association (MAA) hopes the government will consider extending the application for the additional 100 litres of subsidised diesel to multi-purpose vehicle (MPV) owners. In a statement, the association said that currently, the measure is only available to eligible pick-up trucks and sport utility vehicle (SUV) owners. On June 22, the government decided to provide an additional 100 litres of subsidised diesel on top of the existing 200 litres allocation for eligible owners of pick-up trucks and SUVs.

items.title: Homeless man detained after allegedly hurling bricks at police vehicle outside Jalan Hang Lekiu hotel
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Police have arrested a homeless Myanmar national after he damaged a police vehicle by throwing bricks in front of a hotel on Jalan Hang Lekiu here, on Wednesday. Dang Wangi district deputy police chief Supt Nuzulan Mohd Din in a statement today said the man in his 40s, was later arrested with the help of the public at 11.57 pm. Nuzulan said police were continuing their investigation under Section 427 of the Penal Code and Section 6(1)© of the Immigration Act 1959/63.

items.title: No help for Bersatu from PAS election machinery in Johor, Hadi says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — PAS will not deploy its election machinery to help Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia candidates in next month’s Johor state election despite both parties contesting under the Perikatan Nasional (PN) banner, party president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said today. According to Berita Harian , Abdul Hadi said PAS had ended its political cooperation with Bersatu and that the two parties would run separate campaign operations, even though Bersatu had insisted on using the PN logo. “PAS has already terminated its political cooperation with Bersatu.
